items.original_text: We propose pre-finetuning, an additional large-scale learning stage between language model pre-training and fine-tuning. Pre-finetuning is massively multi-task learning (around 50 datasets, over 4.8 million total labeled examples), and is designed to encourage learning of representations that generalize better to many different tasks.
items.claim_text: MUPPET提出了预微调（pre-finetuning），即在大规模预训练语言模型和任务微调之间增加一个大规模多任务学习阶段，使用约50个数据集、超过480万个标注样本，旨在学习能够更好泛化到多种不同任务的表示 [EV-001]
items.audit_note: claim夸大了数据规模：原文说4.8M samples，claim说480万标注样本（正确），但claim还暗示这是纯监督数据，而原文还包含其他类型

items.original_text: We show that pre-finetuning consistently improves performance for pretrained discriminators (e.g. RoBERTa) and generation models (e.g. BART) on a wide range of tasks (sentence prediction, commonsense reasoning, MRC, etc.), while also significantly improving sample efficiency during fine-tuning.
items.claim_text: 预微调一致地提升了预训练判别模型（如RoBERTa）和生成模型（如BART）在广泛任务上的性能，同时显著提高了微调阶段的样本效率 [EV-002]
items.audit_note: claim声称性能随任务数线性提升，但原文说的是在超过临界点后线性提升，claim遗漏了临界点条件

items.original_text: We also show that large-scale multi-tasking is crucial; pre-finetuning can hurt performance when few tasks are used up until a critical point (usually above 15) after which performance improves linearly in the number of tasks.
items.claim_text: 大规模多任务至关重要：当任务数量少于临界点（通常超过15个任务）时，预微调可能损害性能；超过临界点后，性能随任务数量线性提升 [EV-003]

items.original_text: We introduce a new training scheme which uses loss scaling and task-heterogeneous batches so that gradient steps are more evenly balanced across multiple different competing tasks, greatly improving training stability and overall performance.
items.claim_text: MUPPET提出了一种新的训练方案：使用损失缩放（loss scaling）和任务异构批次（task-heterogeneous batches），使梯度步骤在不同竞争任务之间更平衡，大幅提升训练稳定性和整体性能 [EV-004]

items.original_text: In total our multi-task set up learns over 4.8 supervised samples across 4 families of tasks. A full list of all of the datasets we leverage for pre-finetuning is described in appendix §A.1.
items.claim_text: MUPPET的多任务设置涵盖4类任务（分类、摘要、机器阅读理解、常识推理），共使用46个数据集、480万个有监督样本 [EV-005]

items.original_text: We scale data-point loss so that, if the class distribution were uniformly distributed along with our models predictions, all of our losses would have equivalent values. L_i^{scaled}(x_i,y_i;θ) = L_i(x_i,y_i;θ) / log n(i). We found that this static scaling worked incredibly well, outperforming other loss scaling methods in early experimentation.
items.claim_text: MUPPET采用静态损失缩放方法，将每个数据点的损失除以 log n(i)，其中 n(i) 是预测类别数或词汇表大小，该方法在初步实验中优于其他损失缩放方法 [EV-006]

items.original_text: To overcome this, we ensure each batch our model optimizes consists of several tasks. Each worker samples a random batch from our set of tasks and computes a gradient, accumulated for the final update. Empirically we use 64 GPUs for pre-finetuning, resulting in each batch consisting of gradients across 64 sampled tasks.
items.claim_text: MUPPET使用任务异构批次：每个优化步骤的批次包含多个任务的梯度，具体使用64个GPU，每个批次聚合来自64个采样任务的梯度 [EV-007]

items.original_text: Across the board, pre-trained representations that were further refined with pre-finetuning outperformed standard pre-trained representations. We see more modest gains on larger datasets, most likely because we do not need to refine representations beforehand if the fine-tuning dataset is large. On smaller datasets, we see substantial gains. For example, the pre-finetuned RoBERTa-BASE model on RTE improves by close to 9 points, rivaling the RoBERTa-Large accuracy.
items.claim_text: 预微调后的表示在所有任务上优于标准预训练表示。在大数据集上增益较小，在小数据集上增益显著；例如预微调的RoBERTa-Base在RTE上提升了近9个点，达到了RoBERTa-Large的精度 [EV-008]

items.original_text: The pre-finetuned RoBERTa-Large model gets new state-of-the-art on RTE rivaling models an order of magnitude larger than it. We reach a new state of the art on the HellaSwag dataset previously achieved by utilizing a new fine-tuning approach.
items.claim_text: 预微调的RoBERTa-Large在RTE上取得了新的最先进结果，媲美比它大一个数量级的模型；同时在HellaSwag上也达到了新的最先进水平 [EV-009]

items.original_text: We see that the MUPPET variants of our models outperform the baselines consistently across task type and dataset. As a special case we do an in depth analysis of the MUPPET variant of RoBERTa on the notoriously tough ANLI dataset and see the same pattern. Pre-finetuned models consistently outperform their base counterparts.
items.claim_text: MUPPET变体模型在所有任务类型和数据集上一致地超越基线模型，包括在困难的ANLI数据集上，预微调模型始终优于其基础版本 [EV-010]

items.original_text: We see a couple of interesting patterns. First, for individual tasks such as RTE, increasing the pre-finetuning scale monotonically improves performance. ... For other datasets, we see that doing MTL in the < 15 datasets regime is detrimental for end-task finetuning. ... Nevertheless, it seems that as we increase the number of tasks past some critical point, our pre-trained representations become more generalizable. Furthermore, although dependent on the dataset, this critical point is roughly between 10 and 25 tasks.
items.claim_text: 增加预微调的任务数量对RTE等任务单调提升性能；当任务数少于15个时，多任务学习对下游微调有害；超过临界点（约10到25个任务）后，预训练表示变得更具泛化性 [EV-011]

items.original_text: We present our results in Figure 2. We see the importance of properly defining a batching strategy for effective multi-task learning. ... The use of heterogenous batches outperforms other batching strategies by a significant margin and highlights the importance of implementing MTL with the correct batching strategy.
items.claim_text: 异构批次策略（batch heterogeneous）显著优于数据集同质（dataset homogeneous）和批次同质（batch homogeneous）策略，证明了正确批次选择对多任务学习的重要性 [EV-012]

items.original_text: Specifically looking at the 34/40 pre-finetuning scale on Figure 3 we see that we reach higher evaluation accuracies much sooner than the base RoBERTa model (row 0).
items.claim_text: 在低资源实验（QNLI和CoLA）中，使用34-40个任务预微调的模型比基础RoBERTa模型用更少的数据达到更高的评估准确率 [EV-013]

items.original_text: We also show that large-scale multi-tasking is crucial; pre-finetuning can hurt performance when few tasks are used up until a critical point (usually above 15) after which performance improves linearly in the number of tasks. This suggests that previously observed MTL limitations were not fundamental and can instead be attributed to the lack of sufficient scale.
items.claim_text: 先前观察到的多任务学习局限性并非根本性的，而是归因于缺乏足够的规模；当任务数超过临界点（通常超过15）后，性能随任务数线性提升 [EV-014]
